en.extract:
The Bornean forktail (Enicurus borneensis) is a small, black and white bird, with a long and deeply forked tail, in the Old World flycatcher family. It is endemic to the Southeast Asian island of Borneo, where it occupies streamside habitats in montane primary forest.


Taxonomy
It is closely related to the very similar white-crowned forktail (Enicurus leschenaulti) of which it is sometimes considered a subspecies, and from which it was split because of:

a recognizably different type of mitochondrial DNA
the reduced extent of the white forehead shield, which does not cover the crown
its larger size and longer tail with four, rather than five, white tail patches
different calls
different habitat, with the Bornean forktail replacing the lowland dwelling white-crowned forktail in the mountains and in submontane areas.


References

Gill F, D Donsker & P Rasmussen  (Eds). 2022. IOC World Bird List (v12.2). doi :  10.14344/IOC.ML.12.2

The '''Bornean forktail''' (''Enicurus borneensis'') is a small, black and white bird, with a long and deeply forked tail, in the [[Old World flycatcher]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]]. It is [[endemism|endemic]] to the [[Southeast Asia]]n island of [[Borneo]], where it occupies streamside habitats in montane [[old growth forest|primary forest]].<ref name=p&p>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=Phillipps, Karen |year=2011 |series= |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 |pages= |url= }}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
It is closely related to the very similar [[white-crowned forktail]] (''Enicurus leschenaulti'') of which it is sometimes considered a [[subspecies]],<ref>{{cite web |url= http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/speciesfactsheet.php?id=32230|title= Bornean Forktail |access-date=2013-11-16 |work=Species factsheet |first= |last= |publisher=BirdLife International |date=2013 }}</ref> and from which it was [[lumping and splitting|split]] because of:<ref>{{cite journal |author=Moyle, Robert G. |author2=Schilthuizen, Menno |author3=Rahman, Mustafa A. |author4=Sheldon, Frederick H. |year=2005 |title=Molecular phylogenetic analysis of the white-crowned forktail ''Enicurus leschenaulti'' in Borneo |journal= Journal of Avian Biology|volume=36 |issue=2 |pages=96–101 |url=http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/j.0908-8857.2005.03510.x/abstract |doi= 10.1111/j.0908-8857.2005.03510.x|url-access=subscription }}</ref>
* a recognizably different type of [[mitochondrial DNA]]
* the reduced extent of the white forehead shield, which does not cover the [[crown (anatomy)|crown]]
* its larger size and longer tail with four, rather than five, white tail patches
* different calls
* different habitat, with the Bornean forktail replacing the lowland dwelling white-crowned forktail in the mountains and in submontane areas.

==References==
{{Reflist}}
* Gill F, D Donsker & P Rasmussen  (Eds). 2022. IOC World Bird List (v12.2). doi :  10.14344/IOC.ML.12.2
